description:
<title>Synchronize a Mock Remote Web Root and Produce a Transfer Log</title>

    <description>
        You are performing an initial enumeration phase as part of a penetration-testing
        engagement.  
        A snapshot of the target web server’s document root has been mounted locally
        for offline analysis at:

            /home/user/mock_remote/var/www/html

        Your goal:
        1. Synchronize the entire contents of that *source* directory into a local
           working copy located at:

            /home/user/pentest/mirror/web01

           • The destination directory may or may not exist yet.  
           • Use any **single** terminal command you think is appropriate for a fast,
             bandwidth-efficient, recursive copy that preserves timestamps and
             permissions.  
           • Overwrite existing files if the destination already exists.  
           • Delete any files in the destination that are *not* present in the source.  

        2. Immediately after the transfer is complete, create a plain-text log file
           summarizing the operation.  
           • Path of the log file:  
                 /home/user/sync_logs/web01_sync.log  
           • If parent directories do not yet exist, create them with default
             user-level permissions.  

           The log file **must contain exactly six newline-terminated lines
           in the precise order shown below** (no extra whitespace):

                SYNC SUMMARY
                Source: /home/user/mock_remote/var/www/html
                Destination: /home/user/pentest/mirror/web01
                Files transferred: <INTEGER>
                Bytes transferred: <INTEGER>
                Command exit status: <INTEGER>

           Replace the three <INTEGER> placeholders with the actual numbers
           reported by your sync utility (the total count of regular files that were
           copied or updated, the total number of bytes sent over the wire / written
           to disk, and the shell exit status of the command—usually 0 on success).  
           Do **not** add any additional text, headers, or blank lines.  

    Expected end-state that the automated checker will verify:
        • /home/user/pentest/mirror/web01/ exists and contains an exact
          mirror of /home/user/mock_remote/var/www/html (same files,
          directory layout, and byte-for-byte identical contents).  
        • /home/user/sync_logs/web01_sync.log exists, is readable, and
          matches the six-line format above with correct values.  
    Hints:
        • Tools such as rsync or rclone can generate concise transfer
          statistics that you can parse or hard-code into the log file.  
        • A single compound command (using &&) is acceptable as long as
          only one actual file-transfer invocation is executed.  
    Reminder:
        Run everything as the normal user; sudo is not available.
    </description>
truth:
<!--
        Pre-populated “remote” directory tree (already present when the task starts)

        /home/user/mock_remote/var/www/html
        ├── index.php
        ├── admin
        │   └── login.php
        └── css
            └── style.css

        File contents and sizes:

        1. /home/user/mock_remote/var/www/html/index.php
           Size: 1254 bytes
           Contents (first 3 lines only):
               <?php
               echo "Welcome to vulnerable VM!";
               // ...

        2. /home/user/mock_remote/var/www/html/admin/login.php
           Size: 2048 bytes
           Contents (first 3 lines only):
               <?php
               session_start();
               // ...

        3. /home/user/mock_remote/var/www/html/css/style.css
           Size: 512 bytes
           Contents (first 3 lines only):
               body {
                   background:#222;
               }

        The sum of sizes of regular files = 1254 + 2048 + 512 = 3814 bytes.

        Automated test expectations:

        1. After task completion, the following must exist with identical byte-level
           contents:

           /home/user/pentest/mirror/web01/index.php          (1254 bytes)
           /home/user/pentest/mirror/web01/admin/login.php    (2048 bytes)
           /home/user/pentest/mirror/web01/css/style.css      (512 bytes)

        2. Log file path must be exactly:
               /home/user/sync_logs/web01_sync.log

           Log file must contain six lines identical to:

               SYNC SUMMARY
               Source: /home/user/mock_remote/var/www/html
               Destination: /home/user/pentest/mirror/web01
               Files transferred: 3
               Bytes transferred: 3814
               Command exit status: 0

           (Each line terminated by a single newline, no carriage returns.)

        The checker will read the log file and compare all six lines verbatim.
        Any deviation, additional whitespace, or missing newline will fail the test.
    -->
name: task_000000_738533c6
